value.body:
Found during a direct Node API audit of runtime source (`packages/**/src`, excluding tests, scripts, vendor, and generated protocol files). Direct `process.argv`/`exitCode` use in `bin.ts` entrypoints was treated as an intentional Node boundary; this ticket targets shared service/discovery code.

Problem:
CLI/MCP shared helpers still reach for process/global crypto directly despite already depending on Effect `Path`, `FileSystem`, `Config`, and `Stdio` services.

Evidence:
- `packages/cli/src/discovery.ts:71-79` derives runtime discovery paths with `globalThis.process?.getuid?.()`.
- `packages/mcp/src/discovery.ts:79-87` has the same runtime discovery path logic with `globalThis.process?.getuid?.()`.
- `packages/cli/src/services/CliRuntime.ts:57-84` uses Effect `Stdio` but reads `globalThis.process?.stdin?.isTTY` for `stdinIsTTY`.
- `packages/mcp/src/tools/layer.ts:35-40` creates request IDs with global `crypto.randomUUID()` and falls back to `Date.now()`/`Math.random()`.

Suggested remediation:
- Derive the user/runtime suffix from injected env/config instead of `process.getuid`, or provide an explicit process metadata service.
- Extend CLI runtime construction so TTY state comes from `Stdio`/provided IO rather than `globalThis.process`.
- Use Effect `Crypto` for MCP request IDs, with no `Math.random()` fallback in service code.

Acceptance criteria:
- Shared CLI/MCP service code no longer reads `globalThis.process`, global `crypto`, or `Math.random()` for runtime identifiers.
- Entrypoint-only process handling remains isolated in `bin.ts`.
- CLI and MCP discovery path compatibility is preserved.

frontmatter.title: Clean up CLI/MCP shared discovery and request-id Node global usage

value.title: Clean up CLI/MCP shared discovery and request-id Node global usage
